templates.name: Get-Process
templates.description: Gets the processes that are running on the local computer.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Specifies one or more processes by process name. You can specify multiple process names separated by commas and use wildcard characters. Using the `-Name` parameter is optional.

parameters.name: Id
parameters.description: Specifies one or more processes by process ID (PID). You can specify multiple IDs separated by commas. To get the PID of a process, use `Get-Process`. To get the PID of the current PowerShell session, use `$PID`.

parameters.name: InputObject
parameters.description: Specifies one or more **Process** objects. Use a variable that contains the objects, or a command or expression that gets the objects.

parameters.name: IncludeUserName
parameters.description: Indicates that this command adds a **UserName** property to each returned **Process** object.

parameters.name: Module
parameters.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets the modules that the process has loaded. On Windows Vista and later versions of Windows, you must run PowerShell with elevated user rights (**Run as administrator**) to use this parameter on processes that you don't own. Using this parameter is the same as accessing the **Modules** property of each **Process** object. When you use this parameter, `Get-Process` returns a **ProcessModule** object, not a **Process** object. You can't pipe output produced using this parameter to a cmdlet that expects a **Process** object, such as `Stop-Process`. When you use both the **Module** and **FileVersionInfo** parameters together, this cmdlet returns a **FileVersionInfo** object with information about the file version of all modules.

parameters.name: FileVersionInfo
parameters.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets the file version information for the program that runs in the process. On Windows Vista and later versions of Windows, you must run PowerShell with elevated user rights (**Run as administrator**) to use this parameter on processes that you don't own. Using this parameter is the same as accessing the **MainModule.FileVersionInfo** property of each **Process** object. When you use this parameter, `Get-Process` returns a **FileVersionInfo** object, not a **Process** object. You can't pipe output produced using this parameter to a cmdlet that expects a **Process** object, such as `Stop-Process`.

templates.name: Get-Service
templates.description: Gets the services on the computer.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Specifies the service names of services to be retrieved. Wildcards are permitted.

parameters.name: DependentServices
parameters.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets only the services that depend upon the specified service.

parameters.name: RequiredServices
parameters.description: Indicates that this cmdlet gets only the services that this service requires. This parameter gets the value of the **ServicesDependedOn** property of the service.

parameters.name: DisplayName
parameters.description: Specifies, as a string array, the display names of services to be retrieved. Wildcards are permitted.

parameters.name: Include
parameters.description: Specifies, as a string array, a service or services that this cmdlet includes in the operation. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as `s*`. Wildcards are permitted.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.description: Specifies, as a string array, a service or services that this cmdlet excludes from the operation. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as `s*`. Wildcards are permitted.

parameters.name: InputObject
parameters.description: Specifies **ServiceController** objects representing the services to be retrieved. Enter a variable that contains the objects, or type a command or expression that gets the objects. You can pipe a service object to this cmdlet.

templates.name: Restart-Service
templates.description: Stops and then starts one or more services.

parameters.name: Force
parameters.description: Forces the command to run without asking for user confirmation.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Specifies the service names of the services to restart.

parameters.name: InputObject
parameters.description: Specifies **ServiceController** objects that represent the services to restart. Enter a variable that contains the objects, or type a command or expression that gets the objects.

parameters.name: PassThru
parameters.description: Returns an object that represents the service. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

parameters.name: DisplayName
parameters.description: Specifies the display names of the services to restarted. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: Include
parameters.description: Specifies services that this cmdlet restarts. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as s*. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.description: Specifies services that this cmdlet omits. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as s*. Wildcard characters are permitted.

templates.name: Start-Service
templates.description: Starts one or more stopped services.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Specifies the service names for the service to be started. The parameter name is optional. You can use **Name** or its alias, **ServiceName**, or you can omit the parameter name.

parameters.name: InputObject
parameters.description: Specifies **ServiceController** objects representing the services to be started. Enter a variable that contains the objects, or type a command or expression that gets the objects.

parameters.name: PassThru
parameters.description: Returns an object that represents the service. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

parameters.name: DisplayName
parameters.description: Specifies the display names of the services to start. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: Include
parameters.description: Specifies services that this cmdlet starts. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as `s*`. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.description: Specifies services that this cmdlet omits. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as `s*`. Wildcard characters are permitted.

templates.name: Stop-Process
templates.description: Stops one or more running processes.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Specifies the process names of the processes to stop. You can type multiple process names, separated by commas, or use wildcard characters.

parameters.name: Id
parameters.description: Specifies the process IDs of the processes to stop. To specify multiple IDs, use commas to separate the IDs. To find the PID of a process, type `Get-Process`.

parameters.name: InputObject
parameters.description: Specifies the process objects to stop. Enter a variable that contains the objects, or type a command or expression that gets the objects.

parameters.name: PassThru
parameters.description: Returns an object that represents the process. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

parameters.name: Force
parameters.description: Stops the specified processes without prompting for confirmation. By default, `Stop-Process` prompts for confirmation before stopping any process that is not owned by the current user. To find the owner of a process, use the `Get-CimInstance` cmdlet to get a **Win32_Process** object that represents the process, and then use the **GetOwner** method of the object.

templates.name: Stop-Service
templates.description: Stops one or more running services.

parameters.name: Force
parameters.description: Forces the cmdlet to stop a service even if that service has dependent services.

parameters.name: NoWait
parameters.description: Indicates that this cmdlet uses the no wait option.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Specifies the service names of the services to stop. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: InputObject
parameters.description: Specifies **ServiceController** objects that represent the services to stop. Enter a variable that contains the objects, or type a command or expression that gets the objects.

parameters.name: PassThru
parameters.description: Returns an object that represents the service. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

parameters.name: DisplayName
parameters.description: Specifies the display names of the services to stop. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: Include
parameters.description: Specifies services that this cmdlet stops. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as s*. Wildcard characters are permitted.

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.description: Specifies services that this cmdlet omits. The value of this parameter qualifies the **Name** parameter. Enter a name element or pattern, such as s*. Wildcard characters are permitted.
